timestamp_ns.description: Event timestamp in nanoseconds since Unix epoch

item_type.description: BTM launch-item type (es_btm_item_type_t). 'daemon' = system-domain LaunchDaemon (T1543.004), 'agent' = LaunchAgent, 'login_item' = login item.

item_path.description: Filesystem path of the launch item (the plist), from es_btm_launch_item_t.item_url.

executable_path.description: Optional. POSIX executable path from the launchd plist, when available.

legacy.description: True iff a legacy plist-based launch item rather than an SMAppService registration.

managed.description: True iff managed by MDM. Managed daemons are operator-deployed and skipped by detection rules.

uid.description: User ID the item is registered for.

instigator_pid.description: Optional. PID of the process that registered the item (es_event_btm_launch_item_add_t.instigator).

cdhash.description: Code-directory hash (sha1 of the CDDirectory blob) the agent extracts on Hardened-Runtime binaries (issue #68). Optional: NULL for non-HR processes since the ESF target tuple lacks a meaningful cdhash for them. 40 lowercase hex characters when present.

snapshot.description: True for synthetic exec events emitted at extension startup to materialise processes that existed before ESF subscription (issue #11). Detection rules skip snapshot events because they describe historical state, not new attacker activity.

exit_reason.description: Optional. 'host_reconciled' tags exits synthesized by the agent's kill(pid,0) reconciliation pass when the kernel-observed exit event went missing (issue #6 client half). Absent or 'event' means the kernel observed the exit.

snapshot_heartbeat_payload.description: Periodic liveness signal emitted by the agent for PIDs that were materialised via the issue #11 ESF startup snapshot. The server's graph builder updates processes.last_seen_ns on receipt; the TTL reconciler reads COALESCE(last_seen_ns, fork_time_ns) so a snapshot row with fresh heartbeats is exempt from the 6h force-exit (issue #173).

rule_id.description: Stable string identifier of the matched application-control rule (e.g. "app_control:42"). Echoed by the server as the alert's rule_id.

identifier.description: The matched identifier value (e.g. the SHA-256 the BINARY rule matched against).

application_control_undecided_payload.description: Emitted when AUTH_EXEC could not compute a BINARY hash within the kernel deadline budget (or the file was unreadable for the TOCTOU re-stat reasons in FileHashCache.computeSHA256WithDeadline) and the active snapshot's deadline_fallback drove the verdict. Operators consume these events to size their cold-cache rate before flipping the posture between fail-closed / fail-open / audit-only.

verdict.description: Wire verdict the kernel received. "deny" only fires under fail-closed posture; "allow" fires under audit-only (fail-open emits no event by design).

reason.description: Technical cause that prevented a hash decision. "deadline" means the budget headroom was exhausted before chunks finished streaming; "read_failed" means the file could not be opened, fstat failed, or a TOCTOU replace was detected between AUTH and read.

file_size_bytes.description: Size of the executable being authorised. Pairs with reason="deadline" to identify large-binary workloads where the deadline budget is the bottleneck.
